activeShape: explicit translation-object rows with NRSV base, KJV/NABRE addition lanes, and DRB source-address addition lanes

ordinaryDanielNRSV.scope: Daniel 1-12 ordinary Daniel rows only, using Greek Daniel chapter 3 insertion mapping for ordinary Daniel 3:24-30 at active Daniel 3:91-97.
ordinaryDanielNRSV.limitation: This is an internal baseline against trusted canonical Daniel NRSV, not an external NRSV source witness for the additions.

additionKJV.scope: Prayer of Azariah/Song of Three Jews, Susanna, and Bel and the Dragon addition lanes only.

additionNABRE.scope: Prayer/Song, Susanna, and Bel and the Dragon addition lanes only.

additionDRB.scope: Prayer/Song Daniel 3:24-90, Susanna/transition Daniel 13:1-65, and Bel and the Dragon Daniel 14:1-42.

additionNRSV.scope: Prayer of Azariah/Song of Three Jews, Susanna, and Bel and the Dragon NRSV addition text.
additionNRSV.reason: No approved bound NRSV source witness for the additions is present. Oremus candidates are identified but not extracted or approved as bound source witnesses.

fullGreekDanielAllLanes.reason: Greek Daniel cannot be fully trusted while active NRSV addition text remains unverified against an approved source witness.

allowedClaims: Greek Daniel ordinary Daniel 1-12 NRSV is internally baseline-verified against canonical Daniel NRSV under the insertion mapping.
Greek Daniel KJV addition lanes are exact-source-collated for Prayer of Azariah, Susanna, and Bel and the Dragon.
Greek Daniel NABRE addition lanes are exact-source-collated for Prayer/Song, Susanna, and Bel and the Dragon.
Greek Daniel DRB addition/source-address lanes are exact-source-collated under the DRB source-address policy.
forbiddenClaims: Greek Daniel is fully trusted across all lanes.
Greek Daniel NRSV additions are source-verified.
Oremus candidates are bound NRSV source witnesses.
Canonical Daniel internal baseline replaces an external/source witness for NRSV additions.
nextRequiredWork: Acquire or approve NRSV source witnesses for Greek Daniel additions before NRSV addition collation.
Continue Greek Esther source acquisition and address mapping.
Keep deuterocanon/non-Protestant canon status partial until remaining Greek additions and Greek Esther are source-collated or explicitly governed as source-absent.
